title: Introduction to herdr
text: package offers specific functions model primary sources environmental impact livestock based detailed Energy Balance model. accounts energy requirements maintenance, growth, lactation, pregnancy, work, wool production.

previous_headings: What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Enteric fermentation: Emissions produced digestive process based Gross Energy intake diet digestibility. Manure management: Emissions derived Volatile Solids (VSVS) decay, adjusting specific conditions (MCF) diverse management systems (e.g., liquid lagoons, solid storage, pasture, composting).

previous_headings: What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Direct: Emissions manure storage/treatment systems. Indirect: Emissions via nitrogen volatilization (NH3NH_3, NOxNO_x) leaching water systems.

previous_headings: What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Calculates land area (m2m^2) required produce feed, linking animal intake directly crop yields.

previous_headings: What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: herdr allows model different farms, regions, management strategies within project. can compare emissions change farm mountains one valley, two different countries, single run.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: emission calculations within herdr adhere Tier 2 methods established Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change (IPCC). Specifically, use guidelines 2019 Refinement 2006 IPCC Guidelines National Greenhouse Gas Inventories: “Volume 4: Agriculture, Forestry Land Use, Chapter 10: EMISSIONS LIVESTOCK MANURE MANAGEMENT”

title: Introduction to herdr
text: Diet composition primary driver results. functions use nutritional data (Energy, Protein, NDF) determine key parameters like Methane Conversion Factor (YmY_m) Nitrogen Excretion (NexN_{ex}). Automatic Validation: calculating, herdr automatically checks consistency input data, ensuring biologically impossible values flagged prevented.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: Install package directly GitHub using remotes:
code: # Install 'remotes' if necessary if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {     install.packages("remotes") } # Install the herdr package remotes::install_github("JuanCBM99/Animal_GEI_LU")

title: Manure System Guide
text: Based MAT ratio Potential Evapotranspiration (PET) Precipitation (P). Moist: PET / P > 1 Dry: PET / P < 1 Moist: PET / P > 1 Dry: PET / P < 1 Moist: PET / P > 1 Dry: PET / P < 1

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: model case-sensitive like spaces. Always use lowercase underscores (e.g., maize_silage instead Maize Silage).

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: herdr package performs environmental impact assessments based 2019 Refinement 2006 IPCC Guidelines. core model Energy Balance, Gross Energy (GE) intake derived animal’s physiological requirements.

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: NEmNE_m energy required maintain animal’s energy balance without gain loss weight. Formula: NEm=Cfi⋅Weight0.75NE_m = Cf_i \cdot \text{Weight}^{0.75}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: NEaNE_a represents energy used grazing, walking, activities. Cattle: NEa=Ca⋅NEmNE_a = C_a \cdot NE_m (Eq. 10.4) Sheep Goats: NEa=Ca⋅WeightNE_a = C_a \cdot \text{Weight} (Eq. 10.5)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Energy required tissue deposition (weight gain). Cattle Buffalo: (Eq. 10.6) NEg=22.02⋅(BWC⋅MW)0.75⋅WG1.097NE_{g} = 22.02 \cdot \left( \frac{BW}{C \cdot MW} \right)^{0.75} \cdot WG^{1.097} Sheep Goats: (Eq. 10.7) NEg=WGlamb/kid⋅(+0.5b(BWi+BWf))365NE_g = \frac{WG_{lamb/kid} \cdot (+ 0.5b(BW_i + BW_f))}{365}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Energy required milk production based volume fat content. Cattle: NEl=Milk⋅(1.47+0.40⋅Fat)NE_l = \text{Milk} \cdot (1.47 + 0.40 \cdot \text{Fat}) (Eq. 10.8) Sheep Goats: NEl=Milk⋅EVmilkNE_l = \text{Milk} \cdot EV_{milk} (Eq. 10.9)

what: V. Net Energy for Work, Wool, and Pregnancy
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Work (NEworkNE_{work}): NEwork=0.10⋅NEm⋅HoursNE_{work} = 0.10 \cdot NE_m \cdot \text{Hours} Wool (NEwoolNE_{wool}): NEwool=Prwool⋅EVwool365NE_{wool} = \frac{\text{Pr}_{wool} \cdot EV_{wool}}{365} Pregnancy (NEpNE_p): NEp=Cpregnancy⋅NEmNE_p = C_{pregnancy} \cdot NE_m

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Based IPCC Equations 10.14 10.15, derived feed digestibility (DEDE): Maintenance (REMREM): REM=[1.123−(4.092⋅10−3⋅DE)+(1.126⋅10−5⋅(DE)2)−(25.4DE)]REM = \left[ 1.123 - (4.092 \cdot 10^{-3} \cdot DE) + (1.126 \cdot 10^{-5} \cdot (DE)^2) - \left( \frac{25.4}{DE} \right) \right] Growth (REGREG): REG=[1.164−(5.16⋅10−3⋅DE)+(1.308⋅10−5⋅(DE)2)−(37.4DE)]REG = \left[ 1.164 - (5.16 \cdot 10^{-3} \cdot DE) + (1.308 \cdot 10^{-5} \cdot (DE)^2) - \left( \frac{37.4}{DE} \right) \right]

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Gross Energy (GE) total daily energy intake required sustain physiological functions. Formula: GE=(NEm+NEa+NEl+NEwork+NEpREM+NEg+NEwoolREG)DE/100GE = \frac{\left( \frac{NE_m + NE_a + NE_l + NE_{work} + NE_p}{REM} + \frac{NE_g + NE_{wool}}{REG} \right)}{DE/100}

previous_headings: VIII. Methane Module: Enteric Fermentation (CH4CH_4)
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: EF=GE⋅(Ym100)⋅36555.65EF = \frac{GE \cdot \left( \frac{Y_m}{100} \right) \cdot 365}{55.65}

previous_headings: VIII. Methane Module: Enteric Fermentation (CH4CH_4)
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: ET=∑(P)EF(T,P)⋅(N(T,P)106)E_T = \sum_{(P)} EF_{(T,P)} \cdot \left( \frac{N_{(T,P)}}{10^6} \right)

previous_headings: IX. Methane Module: Manure Management (CH4(mm)CH_{4(mm)})
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: VS=[GE⋅(1−DE100)+(UE⋅GE)]⋅[1−ASH18.45]VS = \left[ GE \cdot \left( 1 - \frac{DE}{100} \right) + (UE \cdot GE) \right] \cdot \left[ \frac{1 - ASH}{18.45} \right]

previous_headings: IX. Methane Module: Manure Management (CH4(mm)CH_{4(mm)})
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: EF(T)=(VS(T)⋅365)⋅[B0(T)⋅0.67⋅∑S,kMCFS,k100⋅AWMS(T,S,k)]EF_{(T)} = (VS_{(T)} \cdot 365) \cdot \left[ B_{0(T)} \cdot 0.67 \cdot \sum_{S,k} \frac{MCF_{S,k}}{100} \cdot AWMS_{(T,S,k)} \right]

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions > I. Nitrogen Balance & Excretion
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Nintake=(GE18.45)⋅(CP/1006.25)N_{intake} = \left( \frac{GE}{18.45} \right) \cdot \left( \frac{CP/100}{6.25} \right)

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions > I. Nitrogen Balance & Excretion
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Reflects logic used herdr codebase: * Cattle: Milkprotein=1.9+0.4⋅FatcontentMilk_{protein} = 1.9 + 0.4 \cdot Fat_{content}Nretention=[Milk⋅Milkprotein6.38]+[WG⋅(268−(7.03⋅NEgWG))/10006.25]N_{retention} = \left[ \frac{Milk \cdot Milk_{protein}}{6.38} \right] + \left[ \frac{WG \cdot (268 - (7.03 \cdot \frac{NE_g}{WG})) / 1000}{6.25} \right]

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions > I. Nitrogen Balance & Excretion
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Sheep/Goats: Nexcreted=(Nintake⋅(1−0.1))⋅365N_{excreted} = (N_{intake} \cdot (1 - 0.1)) \cdot 365 Cattle: Nexcreted=(Nintake−Nretention)⋅365N_{excreted} = (N_{intake} - N_{retention}) \cdot 365

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: N2Odirect=N(T,P)⋅Nexcreted⋅Allocation⋅EF3⋅(4428)N_2O_{direct} = N_{(T,P)} \cdot N_{excreted} \cdot Allocation \cdot EF_3 \cdot \left( \frac{44}{28} \right)

previous_headings: XI. N₂O Module: Indirect Emissions > I. Nitrogen Losses from Manure
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Nvol−MMS=∑[(N⋅Nex⋅AWMS)⋅FracGasMS]N_{vol-MMS} = \sum \left[ (N \cdot Nex \cdot AWMS) \cdot Frac_{GasMS} \right]

previous_headings: XI. N₂O Module: Indirect Emissions > I. Nitrogen Losses from Manure
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Nleach−MMS=∑[(N⋅Nex⋅AWMS)⋅FracLeachMS]N_{leach-MMS} = \sum \left[ (N \cdot Nex \cdot AWMS) \cdot Frac_{LeachMS} \right]

previous_headings: XI. N₂O Module: Indirect Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Volatilization: N2OG(mm)=(Nvol−MMS⋅EF4)⋅4428N_2O_{G(mm)} = (N_{vol-MMS} \cdot EF_4) \cdot \frac{44}{28} Leaching: N2OL(mm)=(Nleach−MMS⋅EF5)⋅4428N_2O_{L(mm)} = (N_{leach-MMS} \cdot EF_5) \cdot \frac{44}{28}

previous_headings: XI. N₂O Module: Indirect Emissions > II. Indirect N2ON_2O Calculation (Eq. 10.28/10.29)
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: IPCC (2019). Refinement 2006 IPCC Guidelines National Greenhouse Gas Inventories. Volume 4: AFOLU, Chapter 10.

text: every animal_tag defined census, must now assign “labels” diet. diet_tag: Link animal one diets created Phase 2. works: model sees description (e.g., cattle buffalo) automatically fetches mathematical constants (CaC_a, CfiC_{fi}, etc.) needed energy equations.

previous_headings: 🚀 herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 3: Animal Metabolism
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: must replicate exact combinations animal_tag, region, subregion, class_flex used census assign weights. adult_weight: mature weight adult animal. average_weight: Average weight . Usually calculated : (Winitial+Wfinal)/2(W_{initial} + W_{final}) / 2. weight_gain: Daily weight increase. Calculated : (Wfinal−Winitial)/productive period(W_{final} - W_{initial}) / \text{productive period}.

previous_headings: 🚀 herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 3: Animal Metabolism

Assign Diets: Decide diet_tag applies category livestock_definitions.csv . Check Library: Open ipcc_coefficients.csv, find description best fits animal, copy exact text livestock_definitions.csv.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: goal characterize management systems used livestock group. data allows model derive two primary environmental impacts: Methane (CH4CH_4): Driven anaerobic conditions storage (determined system type, temperature, duration). Nitrous Oxide (N2ON_2O): Driven Nitrogen excretion (NexN_{ex}) calculated Phase 3 specific emission factors system.

previous_headings: 🚀 herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 4: Manure Management (manure_management.csv)
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Follow steps fill manure_management.csv file correctly: Consult Reference Guide: entering data, review Manure System Guide. must use exact system names variants listed , ones recognized IPCC reference database. Sync Tags: Copy animal_tag, region, subregion, class_flex livestock_census.csv. must match exactly. Manage Multiple Systems (Row Duplication): single animal group uses one system (e.g., 60% “Pasture” 40% “Solid Storage”), must create two identical rows group, changing system details allocation value. Assign Systems Climates: row, select appropriate system_base, system_variant, climate_zone following logic provided System Guide.

previous_headings: 🚀 herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 4: Manure Management (manure_management.csv)
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: allocation column defines percentage manure handled specific system. 1.0 Rule: every unique livestock group (tag, region, class), sum allocation column must exactly 1.0 (100%). Example: group uses three different systems, three separate rows group, total allocations (e.g., 0.5 + 0.3 + 0.2) must equal 1.0.

previous_headings: 🚀 herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: final stage herdr engine processes configuration files generate comprehensive environmental impact report.

previous_headings: 🚀 herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 5: Generating Results
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: goal consolidate data populations, nutrition, metabolism, manure management final emission resource-use metrics. model calculate: Total Emissions: CH4CH_4 (Enteric & Manure) N2ON_2O (Direct & Indirect). Carbon Footprint: Total CO2eqCO_2eq based IPCC global warming potentials. Land Use: Total area required based nutritional strategy crop yields.

previous_headings: 🚀 herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 5: Generating Results
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Load Library: Ensure package active R session. Run Assessment: Use generate_impact_assessment() function. function automatically looks user_data/ folder CSV files prepared Phases 1 4. automatic_cycle = TRUE: Use want model calculate offspring herd dynamics based mature animals (Phase 1, Option B). automatic_cycle = FALSE: Use want model stick strictly population numbers entered manually livestock_census.csv.

previous_headings: 🚀 herdr Workflow: From Raw Data to Impact Assessment > Phase 5: Generating Results
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
code: library(herdr)  # Execute the master function # saveoutput = TRUE creates a CSV file with your results in the output folder results <- generate_impact_assessment(   automatic_cycle = FALSE,    saveoutput = TRUE )  # Preview the results head(results)

title: Calculate CH4 Emissions from Manure Management — calculate_CH4_manure

Calculate CH4 Emissions from Manure Management — calculate_CH4_manure
text: automatic_cycle Logical. TRUE, uses built-model automatic farm cycle calculation. Default FALSE. saveoutput TRUE (default) results saved output folder.

what: Summarize CH4, N2O Emissions, and Land Use — generate_impact_assessment
title: Summarize CH4, N2O Emissions, and Land Use — generate_impact_assessment
text: Summarize CH4, N2O Emissions, Land Use

title: Summarize CH4, N2O Emissions, and Land Use — generate_impact_assessment
code: generate_impact_assessment(   automatic_cycle = FALSE,   region = NULL,   subregion = NULL,   animal = NULL,   type = NULL,   class_flex = NULL,   saveoutput = TRUE,   group_by_identification = TRUE )

title: Summarize CH4, N2O Emissions, and Land Use — generate_impact_assessment
text: automatic_cycle Logical. TRUE built-model, FALSE manual livestock_census.csv. region Character/Numeric vector filter. subregion Character vector filter. animal Livestock type (animal_type). type Livestock subtype (animal_subtype). class_flex Management class (e.g., 'grazing', 'stall'). saveoutput TRUE saves output folder. group_by_identification TRUE returns animal_tag.
